strengths: 15+ years of full-stack engineering experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure—meets core seniority requirement
Demonstrated technical leadership through staff-level roles, teaching/workshops, and building products at scale (iOS app top 10 US, GISCollective adopted by Green Map)
Currently working on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks at re:cinq, showing recent pivot toward AI domain
concerns: No evidence of AI specialization or recognized expertise in AI—current role is too recent (2 months) and previous roles show no AI product or platform leadership
Lacks Master's degree or PhD in AI-related field (preferred qualification); background is generalist full-stack engineer, not AI specialist
No demonstrated experience advising customers/partners on cloud or AI adoption strategy; resume shows implementation focus rather than strategic advisory or thought leadership

key_factors.factor: Recent AI infrastructure work vs. proven AI product track record

response.justification: This candidate has strong foundational credentials—appropriate seniority, relevant degree, proven ability to lead teams and architect systems at scale. However, the role explicitly requires 'specialized' expertise in AI strategy with 'demonstrated products or platforms built with AI.' The resume shows a generalist engineer beginning to transition into AI infrastructure work, not an established AI strategist or specialist. The current role at re:cinq is too recent to demonstrate depth or impact on strategic AI decisions. While technically capable of growing into this position, the candidate lacks the recognized AI expertise and strategic advisory experience that define this director-level AI strategy role.
